Summarize the following text: "The Eiffel Tower was completed in 1889 as the centerpiece of the World's Fair held in Paris to celebrate the centennial of the French Revolution. Designed by the engineering firm of Gustave Eiffel, the wrought-iron lattice tower was initially criticized by many artists as an eyesore. At roughly 300 meters, it was the tallest man-made structure in the world until the Chrysler Building surpassed it in 1930. Today it is one of the most visited paid monuments on Earth, drawing millions of tourists each year."

Summarize the following text: "Honeybees communicate the location of food sources to their nestmates through a behavior known as the waggle dance. A returning forager moves in a figure-eight pattern on the honeycomb, and the angle of the dance relative to vertical encodes the direction of the food relative to the sun. The duration of the waggle phase indicates the distance from the hive. The Austrian scientist Karl von Frisch decoded this dance language and received a Nobel Prize in 1973 for his work."

Summarize the following text: "The Suez Canal is an artificial waterway in Egypt that connects the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea, allowing ships to travel between Europe and Asia without sailing around Africa. It opened in 1869 after a decade of construction overseen by the French diplomat Ferdinand de Lesseps. The canal is about 193 kilometers long and handles a significant share of global maritime trade. A major expansion completed in 2015 added a parallel channel along part of its length to increase capacity."

Summarize the following text: "Around the 1440s, Johannes Gutenberg of Mainz developed a printing system based on movable type cast in metal, a screw press, and oil-based ink. His workshop produced the famous Gutenberg Bible around 1455, demonstrating that books could be reproduced quickly and consistently. The technology spread rapidly across Europe, drastically lowering the cost of books. Historians credit the printing press with accelerating the spread of literacy, the Reformation, and the Scientific Revolution."

Summarize the following text: "Mount Everest, the highest mountain on Earth, rises about 8,849 meters above sea level in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and the Tibet region of China. It is known as Sagarmatha in Nepal and Chomolungma in Tibet. The first confirmed ascent was made in 1953 by Edmund Hillary of New Zealand and Tenzing Norgay, a Sherpa mountaineer. Today hundreds of climbers attempt the summit each year, raising concerns about overcrowding and safety."

Summarize the following text: "In 1928, Alexander Fleming noticed that a mold called Penicillium notatum had contaminated one of his bacterial culture plates at St Mary's Hospital in London and was killing the surrounding bacteria. This observation led to the development of penicillin, the world's first widely used antibiotic. Mass production techniques developed during the Second World War made the drug available to treat wounded soldiers. Fleming shared the 1945 Nobel Prize in Medicine with Howard Florey and Ernst Chain for the discovery."

Summarize the following text: "The Trans-Siberian Railway is the longest railway line in the world, stretching about 9,289 kilometers from Moscow to the Pacific port of Vladivostok. Construction began in 1891 under Tsar Alexander III and the main route was completed in 1916. A nonstop journey along the full route takes about seven days. The railway played a crucial role in opening Siberia to settlement and industry, and it remains a vital freight corridor for Russia today."

Summarize the following text: "In May 1997, the IBM supercomputer Deep Blue defeated world chess champion Garry Kasparov in a six-game match, winning by a score of 3.5 to 2.5. It was the first time a computer had beaten a reigning world champion in a match played under standard tournament time controls. Kasparov had won an earlier match against the machine in 1996 before IBM substantially upgraded it. The victory became a landmark moment in the history of artificial intelligence."

Summarize the following text: "The Great Wall of China is a series of fortifications built across northern China over many centuries to protect against raids by nomadic groups from the steppe. Most of the well-preserved sections that tourists visit today were built during the Ming dynasty between the 14th and 17th centuries. A 2012 survey by China's cultural heritage administration estimated the combined length of all wall sections at over 21,000 kilometers. Contrary to popular myth, the wall is not visible to the naked eye from the Moon."

Summarize the following text: "CRISPR is a gene-editing technology adapted from a natural defense system that bacteria use to fight viruses. Scientists pair a guide RNA with the Cas9 enzyme to cut DNA at a precise location, allowing genes to be disabled, repaired, or replaced. Jennifer Doudna and Emmanuelle Charpentier received the 2020 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for developing the method. The technology is now being tested in treatments for genetic disorders such as sickle cell disease, while its potential use in human embryos remains ethically controversial."

Summarize the following text: "In the year 79 AD, the eruption of Mount Vesuvius buried the Roman city of Pompeii under several meters of volcanic ash and pumice. The rapid burial preserved buildings, frescoes, household objects, and even the shapes of victims' bodies in remarkable detail. The city lay largely forgotten until systematic excavations began in the 18th century. Today Pompeii is one of the most important archaeological sites in the world, offering a snapshot of daily life in ancient Rome."

Summarize the following text: "ARPANET, funded by the United States Department of Defense, transmitted its first message between computers at UCLA and the Stanford Research Institute in October 1969. It was the first wide-area network to use packet switching, a technique that breaks data into small blocks that travel independently across the network. In 1983, ARPANET adopted the TCP/IP protocol suite, which became the technical foundation of the modern internet. The network was formally decommissioned in 1990, by which time the internet had far outgrown it."

Summarize the following text: "Each autumn, monarch butterflies from eastern North America undertake a migration of up to 4,800 kilometers to spend the winter in oyamel fir forests in the mountains of central Mexico. No single butterfly completes the round trip; it takes several generations to return north the following spring and summer. Monarch caterpillars feed exclusively on milkweed plants, which makes the loss of milkweed habitat a major threat to the species. Conservation programs now encourage planting milkweed along the migration corridor."

Summarize the following text: "The Hagia Sophia in Istanbul was completed in 537 AD under the Byzantine emperor Justinian I, when the city was known as Constantinople. Its massive central dome was an engineering marvel that influenced religious architecture for centuries. The building served as a Christian cathedral for over 900 years, became a mosque after the Ottoman conquest in 1453, and was converted into a museum in 1935. In 2020, the Turkish government reclassified it as a mosque once again."

Summarize the following text: "Voyager 1, launched by NASA in 1977, flew past Jupiter and Saturn before continuing toward the edge of the solar system. In 2012 it became the first human-made object to enter interstellar space, crossing the boundary where the sun's solar wind gives way to the plasma of the galaxy. The spacecraft carries a Golden Record containing sounds and images intended to portray life on Earth to any intelligent life that might find it. More than four decades after launch, it is the most distant human-made object and still transmits data back to Earth."
